rule: Any KILLED verdict by a current adversarial judge holds the edge. SURVIVES means the judge could not construct a stronger competitor stack or reduce the value to DX, native packaging, or a mechanism with no measured founder outcome.

verdicts.why: Keeping custom-tool outputs out of context while the model drives the loop yields a measured ~27% token cut on a same-answer run, and recreating it on OpenAI/Gemini requires building a whole sandbox-plus-tool-dispatch runtime that no native feature provides, not trivial glue.

The full-loop countdown is self-admitted absence-of-evidence with only a 0-vs-1 tool-call behavioral artifact, easily recreated by tracking token spend and gating/injecting budget warnings, with no measured cost, speed, or reliability outcome a founder pays for.

It is explicitly an observability nicety with no cost win, and the typed miss-reason is trivially derivable by diffing your own prompt prefixes that you already control, so it is just a named field, not a founder outcome.

The claim concedes granularity coarsens to page-level on PDFs and that DIY verbatim-quote plus str.find resolves at parity on frontier models, leaving only no-store convenience, a thinner integration path, and a marginal token quirk that the competent DIY stack matches.

The measured 4.5x cost and setup-call advantage is against a file-search strawman; the real best competitor stack is the model emitting a verbatim quote plus normalized str.find on the same inline chunks, which the citations evidence itself shows reaches frontier-model parity with no store and trivial glue.

The cost/speed delta is confounded by model-tier mismatch (cheap Haiku vs full-frontier gpt-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro) and is config-fragile (only ~8% cheaper at keep=3), so a competitor pairing its own cheap tier like gpt-5-mini or Gemini Flash with native compaction recreates equal-accuracy cost and speed on the same workload.

The fan-out workload shows a real token-cost win with a same-answer gate, and OpenAI/Gemini lack a native stack that lets model-written code loop over developer tools while keeping bulky intermediate outputs out of context without building an equivalent custom sandbox or batch layer.

The exact provider-side countdown is distinctive, but the founder-valued outcome can be recreated with ordinary app-side budget accounting, tool gating, and low-token handoff prompts, so this is mostly native control packaging.

Typed cache miss reasons are useful observability, but a competitor stack can hash and diff model, system, tools, and message prefixes itself, and the evidence shows no measured cost, speed, or reliability win.

Best-to-best competitor stacks can inline or locally extract sources, require structured source IDs or quotes, and resolve citations deterministically, leaving Claude's edge mostly native packaging, token-accounting convenience, and no-store DX rather than unique accuracy or security.

For preselected RAG chunks, the best OpenAI/Gemini stack is inline chunks plus structured citation fields and a quote or span resolver, not hosted file search, so the measured store/setup delta is not a durable capability gap.

A reusable Claude container is nice DX, but competitors can preserve long-idle workspace state with code execution plus Files/File API or object storage and rehydrate it, and the receipt only proves a 31-minute nonce rather than a founder-valued 30-day reliability edge.

The measured cap is real, but emitting over 128k tokens in one batch call is mostly packaging because a competitor can chunk, continue, and stitch the same artifact at similar token cost, while Claude is async and no accuracy or speed win is shown.

Under the stated no-memory long-stream workload, Claude and OpenAI both hit exactness repeatedly but Claude is materially cheaper and faster than the tested OpenAI compaction and Gemini full-context stacks at equal accuracy.

The direct-PDF page quote object is convenient, but file-search/file-search-store or deterministic page extraction plus structured-output citations can produce the founder-valued page-plus-quote result, and the receipt does not compare total cost, latency, or security against that stack.

Native three-way citation typing is nice, but serializing plain text, PDF pages, and search-result chunks into a unified source map and using OpenAI/Gemini structured citations with quote-offset validation recreates the workload without hosted vector stores, so the evidence mainly proves one-call packaging.

Claude's source-quote web citation object is higher fidelity, but competitors can pair URL citations or search results with page fetch, URL-context extraction, and deterministic quote matching, while the receipt shows equal answer and URL-citation success with no measured user-value delta beyond native packaging and token accounting.
